head.title: Public Anthropology
head.description: Danilyn Rutherford’s public writing brings together essays, commentary, and media appearances that address disability, care, and social policy alongside broader reflections on anthropology, ethics, and contemporary life.

intro: A short introduction describing the content of the page, how it is divided into reflections (self-published long-form writings); interventions (opinion essays in popular magazines, newspapers, etc.); and interviews, etc.; and reminding readers that this is popular-facing work, and academic work can be found on a separate page; etc.
reflectionsDescription: An introductory paragraph describing how reflections are long-form writings, somewhat in the spirit of blog posts, integrating material from your popular writing and academic work; how reflections compare to the other content on this page and elsewhere; and any other information the visitor should be aware of.
interventionsDescription: An introductory paragraph describing how interventions are online essays/opinion pieces for a popular audience, formally published with a third-party magazine, newspaper, etc.
interviewsDescription: A short introduction describing how interviews encompass podcasts, radio appearances, etc. meant for a popular audience.
